activities_projects.title: Universal Adult Franchise Debate
activities_projects.objective: Evaluate the difference between political and economic equality
steps: Re-read Kanta’s story from the textbook
Identify two moments: one where she feels equal (the voting line) and one where she feels unequal (the hospital line)
Conduct a debate on: 'Is voting rights enough to make a person equal in India?'
Summarize the impact of poverty on the exercise of democratic rights
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Analyzes why political equality (voting) does not always lead to social or economic equality
activities_projects.evaluation: Strength of arguments regarding the gap between law and reality (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Article 15: The Legal Shield
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the specific protections offered by the Indian Constitution
materials: Copy of Article 15 from the NCERT book
steps: List the five grounds on which the State cannot discriminate (Religion, Race, Caste, Sex, Place of Birth)
Research a real-life scenario where a public place (like a well or park) was denied to someone
Write a 'Letter to the Editor' explaining how such denial violates Article 15
Discuss why 'Place of Birth' is an important ground for equality
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Demonstrates a clear understanding of the legal framework against discrimination
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy in applying Article 15 to real-world scenarios (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Midday Meal Data Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Investigate the social and educational impact of government programs
materials: Internet access for school attendance data
steps: Research how the Midday Meal scheme affected the enrollment of girl students in schools
Explain how this scheme reduces 'Caste Prejudices' (children eating together, Dalit mothers being employed as cooks)
Create a bar graph showing the hypothetical increase in attendance before and after the scheme
Discuss the challenges still faced in implementing the scheme effectively
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the dual role of government schemes in providing nutrition and social justice
activities_projects.evaluation: Graphical representation and analysis of social benefits (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Dignity and the 'Ansari' Case Study
activities_projects.objective: Examine how religious discrimination affects an individual's dignity
materials: Case study of Mr. and Mrs. Ansari from the textbook
steps: Analyze why the Ansaris were refused an apartment despite having the money
Discuss the suggestion given to them to change their names and why they refused
Relate this incident to the concept of 'Dignity' and 'Self-respect'
Write a short essay on: 'Why treating everyone with respect is essential for a democracy'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Explains how social identity (religion) can lead to inequality even in an economically sound background
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of reflection on the link between identity and dignity (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Civil Rights Movement Comparison
activities_projects.objective: Compare the struggle for equality in India and the USA
steps: Create a table comparing the Civil Rights Act (1964) in the US and the Indian Constitution (1950)
Identify common themes (discrimination in public transport/places)
Discuss why laws alone are not enough to change people's 'mindsets'
Present a brief talk on 'Global Struggles for Equality'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies that the challenge of equality is a global phenomenon that requires both law and social change
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity of comparison and understanding of the role of 'mindsets' (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The 'Rule of Law' Inquiry
activities_projects.objective: Understand the meaning of 'Equality Before Law'
steps: Imagine a situation where a high-ranking official and a common citizen commit the same traffic violation
Explain what would happen under a 'Rule of Law' system
Identify which provision of the Indian Constitution ensures this equality
Discuss obstacles that prevent poor people from getting equal justice in courts
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Defines the 'Rule of Law' and identifies barriers to its implementation
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical consistency in the legal argument provided (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Classroom Diversity Audit
activities_projects.objective: Recognize and celebrate diversity as a component of equality
steps: Conduct a survey of the different languages spoken at home by classmates
List the different festivals celebrated by the students
Discuss if anyone feels 'excluded' during certain activities
Propose three steps to make the classroom more inclusive for everyone
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Promotes the idea that equality involves respecting and including diversity
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of the inclusion proposal and survey conduct (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: B.R. Ambedkar: The Architect of Equality
activities_projects.objective: Study the philosophy of self-respect in a democracy
materials: Excerpts from Dr. Ambedkar’s writings in the textbook
steps: Read the section on Ambedkar’s views on self-respect
Analyze his statement: 'It is disgraceful to live at the cost of one’s self-respect'
Design a commemorative stamp that highlights his fight against untouchability
List two laws passed in India to protect the rights Ambedkar fought for
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects the life of Dr. Ambedkar to the constitutional abolition of untouchability
activities_projects.evaluation: Creative design and historical accuracy (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Media and Equality Project
activities_projects.objective: Observe how equality is portrayed in news and advertisements
steps: Find one news story about an 'inequality' (e.g., lack of water in a slum)
Find one advertisement that shows 'equality' or 'inclusion'
Analyze if the media gives enough space to the problems of the poor
Prepare a 2-minute 'Equality News Bulletin' for the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Critically examines the role of media in highlighting or ignoring inequality
activities_projects.evaluation: Analysis of media bias and presentation of the bulletin (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The 'Changing Mindsets' Campaign
activities_projects.objective: Address the challenge of slow social change
steps: The textbook states that 'attitudes change very slowly'. Identify a common prejudice (e.g., regarding gender or work)
Create a slogan that challenges this prejudice
Plan a 'Street Play' (Nukkad Natak) script that shows a person standing up for their dignity
Explain why democratic societies need to keep struggling for equality
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes that democracy is a 'continuous struggle' and not a finished product
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity of the campaign and understanding of social attitudes (10 marks)
